'''Lee Andrew Gilmour''' (born 12 March 1978) is an English [[assistant coach]] at [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] and a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He played in the [[Super League]] for the [[Wigan Warriors]] (with whom he won 1998's [[Super League III]]), the [[Bradford Bulls]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] (with whom he won 2006's [[Super League XI]]), the [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]]. Gilmour represented [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] at international level.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Gilmour was born in [[Dewsbury]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
Gilmour started his career with Shaw Cross Sharks, had a spell at [[Thornhill Trojans]], and played for Dewsbury Moor before signing for [[Wigan Warriors]]. He toured New Zealand with Great Britain Academy in 1997. Gilmour played at {{rlp|sr}} for Wigan in their [[1998 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos]]. Gilmour played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] from the interchange bench in their [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].

Gilmour went on to play for [[Bradford Bulls]], the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]],<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> [[England national rugby league team|Emerging England]],<ref>{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|title=Elder statesmen negotiate England escape|newspaper=The Times|date=20 July 1998|page=32|publisher=News International Trading Limited|location=London|id={{ProQuest|317976921}}}}</ref> and [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]. As [[Super League VI]] champions, the Bradford Bulls played against [[2001 NRL season|2001 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[2002 World Club Challenge]]. Gilmour played at centre in Bradford's victory. Gilmour played for [[Bradford Bulls]] from the bench in their [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].
Gilmour played for St Helens from the interchange bench in their [[2006 Challenge Cup]] Final victory against the [[Huddersfield Giants]]. St Helens reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], and Gilmour played as a {{rlp|sr}} in Saints' 26-4 victory.
Gilmour gained true international repute when during the [[2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations]] he scored the decisive try in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Lions']] 23-12 win in [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Aussie Stadium]] in 2006. 
As 2006 Super League champions, St Helens faced 2006 NRL Premiers the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2007 World Club Challenge]]. Gilmour played as a {{rlp|sr}} in Saints' 18-14 victory.
He played in 2008's [[2008 Super League Grand Final|Super League XIII Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-04|access-date=2008-10-05}}</ref>

In July 2009 it was announced that Gilmour had signed a two-year contract with Super League rivals, [[Huddersfield Giants]].

He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Stewart|first=Rob|title=Lee Smith targets place in England rugby union team after Grand Final victory|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/6299292/Lee-Smith-targets-place-in-England-rugby-union-team-after-Grand-Final-victory.html|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=The Telegraph|date=12 October 2009}}</ref>

On 23 June 2011 Gilmour signed a new two-year deal with [[Huddersfield Giants]] despite talks of his retirement.<ref>[http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=22628', 23 June 2011.]</ref>

In June 2012 Gilmour negotiated an early release from his Huddersfield Giants contract and went on to sign a two-year deal with the [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/18630710', Gilmour Move to Castleford.]{{dead link|date=September 2025|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

In May 2014, Gilmour joined [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] on loan. In September 2014, he was appointed as a coach at Wakefield.

On 22 July 2016, Gilmour signed for his home town amateur club Shaw Cross Sharks where he started his career to play in the prestigious amateur RL competition in National Conference Division 1 until the end of the 2016 season.
